title: Disciphania peltata
intro: Disciphania peltata, also known as Wild Pepper, is a plant species in the family Menispermaceae, native to the tropical regions of Africa and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties, which can help alleviate fever, reduce pain, and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of Disciphania peltata include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Disciphania peltata is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, often combined with other herbs to create remedies for various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Disciphania peltata is classified within the family Menispermaceae. This family belongs to the order Ranunculales and the class Magnoliopsida. The class Magnoliopsida is a subgroup of the larger group called Angiosperms, which are flowering plants. The order Ranunculales is a part of the larger group called Eudicots, which is a division of flowering plants. Disciphania peltata is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.
